character_dossiers.name: the captain
character_dossiers.biography: A weak, drunken sailor with a cutlass, suffering from illness and delirium. He is the subject of the narrator's account and dies from apoplexy.

character_dossiers.name: Black Dog
character_dossiers.biography: A shipmate of the captain, described as a figure seen near the inn.

character_dossiers.name: the blind beggar
character_dossiers.biography: A blind, aged man in a tattered cloak who interacts with the narrator and the captain, demanding assistance.

character_dossiers.name: Doctor Livesey
character_dossiers.biography: A physician mentioned as a potential contact for help.

character_dossiers.name: my mother
character_dossiers.biography: The narrator's parent, who accompanies him in seeking help after the captain's death.

character_dossiers.name: Jim
character_dossiers.biography: The first-person narrator of the account.

character_dossiers.name: Mrs. Crossley
character_dossiers.biography: A resident of the hamlet who offers a pistol and assistance to the narrator and his mother.

character_dossiers.name: the doctor
character_dossiers.biography: A physician who was absent when the captain died.

character_dossiers.name: Bill
character_dossiers.biography: A name used by the blind beggar to refer to the captain.
